title: trifolium ochroleucon subsp. roseum
intro: Trifolium ochroleucon subsp. roseum, also known as the white clover, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of various flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Trifolium ochroleucon subsp. roseum is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
